title: Spiritual healing in palliative care with clinical hypnosis: neuroscience and therapy.
journal_full: The American journal of clinical hypnosis
abstract: The neuroscientific features of inner consciousness, including its role in suffering and in accessing states of mind that relieve suffering; details salient meditative and hypnotic approaches appropriate for palliative settings of care; discusses core principles and orientations shared by effective approaches; and proposes early integration of hypnotic training as a coping skill and a platform for spiritual exploration, as desired.
